title: Writing - Part 1: Technology in Education
description: Your class has been discussing the role of technology in modern schools.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the roles in your notes. You should explain which role you consider more important, giving reasons for your opinion.

roles: Preparing students for the digital world
opinions: Technology distracts students more than it helps them.
Digital skills are essential for future employment.
Traditional teaching methods are more effective.

structure.introduction: Introduce technology in education and the two roles you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first role (e.g., access to online resources) and its advantages.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second role (e.g., preparing students for the digital world) and why it matters.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both roles and state which you consider more important.

content.modelAnswer:
Technology has transformed almost every aspect of modern life, and education is no exception. Schools today use a wide range of digital tools, from interactive whiteboards to online learning platforms. This essay will examine how technology provides access to vast resources and how it prepares students for a digital future.

The internet gives students immediate access to an enormous range of educational materials. Whether researching a topic for homework or watching an instructional video, learners can find information quickly and easily. This encourages independent study and allows students to explore subjects at their own pace, which is particularly beneficial for those who need extra support.

Furthermore, using technology at school prepares young people for the modern workplace. Almost every job today requires some level of digital competence, from basic computer skills to using specialised software. Students who grow up comfortable with technology are better equipped to succeed professionally.

In conclusion, while both benefits are significant, I believe preparing students for the digital world is ultimately more important. Access to resources is helpful, but without the skills to navigate and use technology effectively, those resources are of limited value. Schools should therefore prioritise digital literacy as a core part of the curriculum.
